content: I would first check with your email provider's online access, as many have strong filters that can eliminate such mail before thunderbird sees them - I also suggest creating filters that focus on domain name aspect of the email, as the big name spammers use their corporate email domains. - if you have a filter that does not appear to be working. please post the email address you are trying to block and a screenshot of your filter. thank you
creator: davidsk

content:
Thanks so much David.

For example: yourwelcomemarriot@lolfeaturing.garden marriotwelcome@commandertwenty.space hellofrommarriot@whereverhas.garden marriotwelcomenote@africanthreatened.lat welcometomarriot@cnbowu.com marriotgreeting@khonkaenproperties.com

To get around this I have set up filters within Tbird not just for “Sender contains…” but also for “Subject contains…” but that hasn’t worked.
creator: kitty6

content:
Use 'Match any' instead of 'Match all'. Trying to fight spam using static filters is futile. Using TB built-in spam mail controls, and training the filter for what's spam, and what's not spam is your best bet. https://support.mozilla.org/kb/thunderbird-and-junk-spam-messages

Note, unless spam messages already get blocked on your email provider's server, once they are received by Thunderbird, all your filter can do is to move them to the Spam folder, so that your Inbox will not get clogged with spam.
creator: christ1

content: I suggest making the folder focus just on the minimum, such as the domain name, e/g.. @xxxx and avoid the full email address. And I think you want filter for ANY, not ALL. That is, one of your filters i for omaha AND steak AND marriott. Do you really require all three words in the subject? Or any one of them? Your other filter requires the FROM to be from bluecross AND from marriot AND from Ace And from Club - that will never be true. You probably meant ANY, not ALL.
creator: davidsk

content:
Some e-mail service providers have levels of spam filter aggressiveness that one can select. You may be able to reduce the quantity of spam messages simply by choosing the next highest level.

Some providers have additional tools such as Sieve that can be scripted to block spam. They require some technical knowledge.
creator: mozilla98
